extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb sprinted off BENNETT Sam's wheel inside final meters for stage win while crosswind echelon split mid-race failed due to non-cooperation between YATES Adam and POGACAR Tadej groups
tactical_patterns: Mid-race echelon split failed because chasers including Pogacar and Yates groups refused cooperation, allowing peloton reunification by 41km.
Pogacar's team controlled pace after Yates crash at 41km to prevent time gaps despite no GC threat remaining.
Ewan positioned on Bennett's wheel through leadout, suggesting sprinters targeting two-time stage winner as reference point rather than own teams.

rider_signals.YATES Adam: neutral: crashed heavily at 41km requiring medical car assistance but returned to peloton with Pogacar's team controlling tempo
rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: first 2021 World Tour win by explicitly drafting Bennett rather than following Lotto-Soudal leadout shows tactical flexibility
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: neutral: made echelon split but chose not to cooperate with chase despite 35-second GC lead suggesting conservative final-stage tactics
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: won two prior stages but became reference wheel for rivals, allowing Ewan superior final positioning
